name: Toffee Crunch-Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Insert metal blade into the dry processor.
2. Add butter, shortening, brown sugar, and granulated sugar to the processor.
3. Process until smooth, about 15 seconds.
4. Add egg and vanilla; process until smooth, about 20 seconds.
5. Add flour, oats, salt, baking powder, chopped candy, and pecans to the processor.
6. Pulse until dough is mixed and pecans are chopped.
7. Transfer mixture to a bowl and stir in chocolate pieces.
8. Drop by heaping teaspoons onto greased baking sheets, 2 inches apart.
9. Adjust oven rack to middle position.
10. Bake 1 sheet at a time at 375°F for 8 minutes.
11. Check for doneness as bottoms brown easily; bake 2 minutes longer or until golden.
12. Cool slightly before removing cookies from the baking sheet.
13. Cool thoroughly on wire racks.
14. Makes 3-1/2 dozen.
Tips:
For creaming in a food processor, add shortening (or melted butter or oil) to eggs while the machine is running. Sugar is then added to the egg mixture. Nuts are most efficiently chopped and mixed into batters simultaneously using half-second pulses. To avoid cookies that become tough from overprocessing, always use half-second pulses to incorporate dry ingredients.
